ascending_order.label: Ascending and Descending Sorts
ascending_order.summary: Choose ASC or DESC for text, numeric, and date columns.

sketch0.title: ASC Moves from Smaller to Larger

sc_asc_meaning.title: Meaning of ASC
sc_asc_meaning.prompt: What does `ASC` do in `ORDER BY price ASC`?
sc_asc_meaning.hint: Think about whether the smallest or largest price should appear first.

help.concept: `ASC` means ascending order, so SQL sorts values from lower to higher, earlier to later, or A to Z depending on the column type.
help.hint_1: For a price column, compare how 6.75 and 129.00 would be arranged.
help.hint_2: The first row after sorting should have the lower price, not the higher one.

options.a: It sorts prices from lowest to highest.
options.b: It sorts prices from highest to lowest.
options.c: It filters out prices below a value.
options.d: It shows only the first price in the table.

sc_default_asc.title: Default sort direction
sc_default_asc.prompt: If you write `ORDER BY price` without `ASC` or `DESC`, what happens in this course's SQL examples?
sc_default_asc.hint: Think about the default direction when no keyword is added.

help.concept: When no direction is written, SQL uses ascending order by default in these beginner examples.
help.hint_1: Compare `ORDER BY price` with `ORDER BY price ASC`.
help.hint_2: The result order matches the version that places smaller prices before larger ones.

options.c: It causes an error because a direction is required.

mc_columns_for_asc.title: Columns that can use ascending order
mc_columns_for_asc.prompt: Which columns from the `products` table can be meaningfully sorted with `ORDER BY ... ASC`? Choose all that apply.
mc_columns_for_asc.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove choices from unrelated topics.
help.hint_2: Choose the option that directly matches the question without relying on answer wording.

mc_asc_examples.title: Queries that sort ascending
mc_asc_examples.prompt: Which queries sort the `products` rows in ascending order? Choose all that apply.
mc_asc_examples.hint: Look for queries that either say `ASC` or rely on the default ascending behavior.

help.concept: Ascending sorting can be written explicitly with `ASC` or left implicit by using `ORDER BY column` with no direction.
help.hint_1: Check which options use `ORDER BY price ASC` or just `ORDER BY price`.
help.hint_2: Avoid the option that uses `DESC`, because that reverses the order.

options.a: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price ASC;
options.b: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price;
options.c: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;
options.d: SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE price ASC;

dr_query_order_basic.title: Build an ascending sort query
dr_query_order_basic.prompt: Arrange the SQL parts to create a query that shows product names and prices sorted by price from lowest to highest.
dr_query_order_basic.hint: Start with `SELECT`, then the table, then the sort clause.

help.concept: A basic SQL query follows a clause order: `SELECT`, `FROM`, then `ORDER BY`.
help.hint_1: The sorting clause comes after the table name.
help.hint_2: The last part should mention `price ASC` to set the direction.

dr_query_order_limit.title: Build a cheapest-products query
dr_query_order_limit.prompt: Arrange the SQL parts to return the 2 cheapest products by price.
dr_query_order_limit.hint: Read each piece and arrange them in the order the statement should be understood.

help.concept: The pieces should form a valid statement in a logical order.
help.hint_1: Start with the piece that introduces the idea or action.
help.hint_2: Place dependent pieces after the part they describe or complete.

fb_sort_keyword.title: Complete the sort direction
fb_sort_keyword.prompt: Fill in the missing keyword to sort prices from lowest to highest.
fb_sort_keyword.hint: Read the sentence around the blank and decide what role the missing word plays.

help.concept: The blank should be filled with the term that makes the statement accurate.
help.hint_1: Look at the words before and after the blank to infer what kind of term is needed.
help.hint_2: Choose the option that completes the statement with the clearest meaning.

fb_sort_keyword.template: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price [blank1];

fb_column_choice.title: Choose the sorted column
fb_column_choice.prompt: Fill in the missing column so the query sorts products by stock from smallest to largest.
fb_column_choice.hint: Use the column that stores how many items are available.

help.concept: The column named in `ORDER BY` is the one SQL uses to arrange the rows.
help.hint_1: In this table, the quantity on hand is stored in `the missing term`.
help.hint_2: Choose the column with values like 25, 10, 8, 50, 15, and 3.

fb_column_choice.template: SELECT name, stock FROM products ORDER BY [blank1] ASC;

ci_sort_prices_asc.title: Sort all products by price ascending
ci_sort_prices_asc.prompt: Write a query that shows `name` and `price` from the `products` table, sorted by `price` in ascending order. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
ci_sort_prices_asc.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the table name and selected columns in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the results table.

ci_sort_prices_asc.starterCode:
-- Show name and price from the products table, sorted by price in ascending order.
SELECT name, price
FROM products
;

try_ascending_order_sketch0.title: Sort Inventory from Lowest Stock
try_ascending_order_sketch0.prompt: Return `id`, `name`, and `stock` from `products`, sorted from the lowest stock value to the highest.

help.concept: The same ASC/DESC rule applies to text, numbers, and sortable date text.
help.hint_2: Run the query and verify that the returned columns and rows match the request.

ascending_examples.title: ASC Moves from Smaller to Larger
ascending_examples.bodyMarkdown:
`ASC` sorts values in ascending order. For text, that is alphabetical order; for numbers, lowest to highest; for ISO dates, earliest to latest.

```sql
SELECT
    name,
    price
FROM products
ORDER BY name ASC;
```

The product names appear alphabetically. Although `ASC` is the default, writing it can make the intention clearer while learning.

descending_examples.title: DESC Reverses the Sequence
descending_examples.bodyMarkdown:
`DESC` sorts in the reverse direction.

```sql
SELECT
    name,
    created_at
FROM products
ORDER BY created_at DESC;
```

The newest date appears first. Choose the direction from the business question: “cheapest first” uses ascending price, “newest first” uses descending date, and “highest stock first” uses descending stock.
